run_summary: Screen 1 has 6 open positions at 8-9 days held, all trading Anthropic/Google AI-event sympathy fades with intact theses and mixed recovery. New flags span Anthropic-ban and Google-Gemini triggers, yielding 15 flags across multiple sectors; applying the basket rule (3+ flags per trigger), most tier as exploratory. Four flags fail BUY eligibility outright; two are conviction candidates after exemption from basket consolidation.

entries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker cannot unlock a narcotic cabinet or dispense Schedule II controlled substances. OMCL's physical robotics moat in hospital pharmacies is essentially zero overlap with consumer AI. Move on average volume but no company news — pure thematic fade.
entries.what_kills: Hospital customer capital budget compression; a major customer cancellation; deterioration in SaaS retention.
entries.reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger (second flag on OMCL, first was Anthropic-Fable). threat_assessment='none' (not 'minimal'), confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. 10-K Risk Factors treat AI as peripheral; core business is DEA Schedule II-compliant pharmacy robotics—zero mechanical link to consumer OS. No company-specific news; thematic fade only. Move -3.51%, five-day +0.84% (positive, light volume). OMCL is unique in 7-flag Google-Gemini basket: threat='none' (sharper than 'minimal'), filings evidence is explicit and concrete (Schedule II compliance moat), and this is the second flag on the name (repositioning confidence). Promotion from exploratory to conviction is justified: OMCL's moat is stronger than MANH/BLKB, and the basket rule allows one conviction-tier name from a basket when filings evidence is materially stronger. Position sizing 20% (confidence 4).

red_team.weakest_link: The promotion to conviction tier rests on OMCL having threat='none' (versus peers' 'minimal'), but the five-day move is already positive (+0.84%), suggesting the market has already priced in the non-threat, leaving limited upside from the current 'exploratory test' framing.
red_team.critique: OMCL's Schedule II compliance moat is genuinely strong—Gemini cannot dispense narcotics. However, the flag is promoted to conviction tier while simultaneously described as an 'exploratory test position' sized at 6%, which is contradictory sizing for a conviction call. More critically, the five-day change is +0.84% (positive), meaning the market has already begun recovering from the sympathy fade; entering at -3.51% on a one-day basis when the five-day trend is already positive may mean the best entry is already passed. The 1.06x volume is also light, suggesting conviction entry may not be warranted at current levels.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
entries.thesis: An Anthropic model ban has zero mechanical bearing on automated photo enforcement of red-light, speed, and bus-lane violations. The product is a regulated physical-evidence chain with multi-year municipal contracts; LLMs don't issue tickets or appear in traffic court. The real Verra risk factor is Commercial Services renewal pricing, which is a 2026 contract negotiation issue, not an AI displacement story.
entries.what_kills: More jurisdictions ban automated enforcement (Ontario precedent), or the in-negotiation Commercial Services customer walks. These are real risks but UNRELATED to today's trigger.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Anthropic-ban trigger; threat_assessment='minimal' (not 'none'), confidence=4. Automated traffic enforcement is mechanistically untouched by Fable model release; regulated physical-evidence chain with multi-year municipal contracts. Real VRRM risk is unrelated Commercial Services contract renewal. One-day move -5.73%, five-day -1.33%, volume 0.92x (not elevated). Part of 3-flag Anthropic-ban basket (VRRM, NCNO, TYL); basket rule applies → exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes Commercial Services contract renewal risk is the real threat, but provides no evidence of actual renewal negotiations or timing, leaving the true catalyst risk unquantified.
red_team.critique: The flag correctly identifies that an Anthropic model ban has no mechanical bearing on traffic enforcement systems. However, the bull case leans heavily on 'real risk is Commercial Services renewal' without disclosing the renewal timeline, customer identity, or any recent negotiation signals. If the renewal was recently secured on favorable terms, the thesis strengthens; if it's at genuine risk, the move may be justified on fundamentals unrelated to today's AI trigger. The 5-day move of -1.33% and volume of 0.92x suggest minimal panic, but the lack of specificity on renewal status weakens conviction.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
entries.thesis: An Anthropic model ban has no mechanical pathway to Tyler revenue. Government procurement cycles run years; CIOs at the county-clerk level are not replacing the court-record system of record with an LLM, especially in a regulatory climate where the federal government just banned a frontier model on security grounds. The trigger arguably HELPS Tyler by reinforcing 'AI is risky for public sector' narratives. Elevated volume (2x) and a sharp drop on a thematic catalyst is the classic sympathy-fade signature.
entries.what_kills: A flagship state announces switching off a Tyler court or ERP system to an AI-native alternative. No evidence of this in filings or news.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Anthropic-ban trigger; threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. Government procurement moat is real—long sales cycles, multi-year contracts, no mechanical link from Fable ban to Tyler revenue. Elevated volume (2.05x) and -3.06% move on irrelevant trigger. Part of 3-flag Anthropic-ban basket (VRRM, NCNO, TYL) → basket rule applies, default to exploratory tier despite strong threat/calibration signals.

red_team.weakest_link: Elevated volume (2.05x) on a 5-day decline of -5.61% suggests institutional selling beyond pure sympathy, and the bull case does not address whether govtech procurement cycles have actually accelerated in this rate-environment.
red_team.critique: The thesis that government procurement cycles insulate Tyler from an AI ban is structurally sound, and the argument that the ban reinforces risk-aversion is clever. However, the 2.05x volume on a 5-day drop of -5.61% is more severe than typical sympathy fade and may indicate selling unrelated to the trigger—such as sector-wide SaaS headwinds or specific procurement deal delays not disclosed in the flag. The bull case does not distinguish between sympathy-fade volume and fundamental repricing volume.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Google Home speakers don't compete with enterprise warehouse management systems. MANH's moat is multi-year deployments integrating with physical warehouse hardware and ERP systems — switching cost is the moat. Already extended over five days, suggesting broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler announcing an AI-native WMS replacement; a Tier-1 retailer publicly migrating off MANH to an LLM-orchestrated supply chain platform; further extension of the 5-day decline on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini trigger (Google Home smart speaker); threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. Enterprise WMS is not a consumer voice-assistant product—multi-year deployments, deep ERP/hardware integration, 9-12 month sales cycles. Move -5.24%, five-day -9.0% on weak relevance. Part of 7-flag Google-Gemini basket (MANH, BLKB, PAYC, PCTY, OSPN, DUOL, OMCL) → basket rule applies. Part of broader basket: default MANH to exploratory.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day decline of -9.0% is material and described as 'already extended,' yet the bull case classifies this as broad SaaS de-rating without confirming whether MANH's WMS-specific bookings or churn data released recently justify a deeper discount than peers.
red_team.critique: While the thesis that Google Home does not compete with warehouse management systems is correct, a -9.0% five-day move is substantial and the bull case attributes it to 'broad SaaS de-rating' without evidence. If MANH has underperformed relative to broader SaaS peers (e.g., NET, CRM) over the same period, the move may reflect specific weakness in vertical SaaS or WMS renewal trends rather than pure sympathy. The flag should have checked MANH's 5-day performance versus SaaS peers to confirm the 'broad de-rating' claim.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't replace nonprofit fundraising / donor management workflows with embedded payment processing and 20+ years of historical donor data. Blackbaud's own filings explicitly warn that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — this is that pattern.
entries.what_kills: A nonprofit-focused AI startup gaining material traction with enterprise nonprofits; renewal-rate compression in upcoming quarter; new Security Incident developments.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini / smart-home trigger; threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. Nonprofit fundraising/donor-data moat is untouched by consumer OS feature; Blackbaud's own filings flag that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals. Part of 7-flag Google-Gemini basket → basket rule applies, exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case cites Blackbaud's own Risk Factors warning about AI sentiment driving volatility, but does not verify whether recent earnings or guidance changes actually warrant the -5.54% five-day move beyond sentiment.
red_team.critique: The flag's invocation of Blackbaud's own Risk Factors disclosure is elegant, but circular—it confirms that AI sentiment *can* drive volatility, not that this volatility is unjustified. If Blackbaud reported weaker-than-expected nonprofit customer renewal rates, net revenue retention compression, or guidance cuts in recent earnings or updates, the -5.54% five-day move would be grounded in fundamentals, not just sentiment. The bull case assumes the move is pure panic without confirming recent earnings beat or unchanged guidance.

entries.name: OneSpan Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't authenticate banking customers or sign legal documents. OSPN's regulated authentication and e-signature business is bound to PSD2/eIDAS standards. The move is small but on lighter volume, classic indiscriminate basket selling.
entries.what_kills: A major bank publicly choosing an AI-native authentication vendor over OSPN; FIDO Alliance changes that disadvantage OSPN.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger; threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. Regulated FIDO2/PSD2-standard authentication and e-signature are not displaced by consumer OS multitasking features. Core business is standards-bound, not AI-vulnerable. Move -3.72%, five-day -2.83%, volume 0.87x (light). Part of 7-flag Google-Gemini basket → exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case asserts that standards-bound regulated software is 'structurally insulated' from AI product launches, but does not address whether OneSpan's own 10-K discloses AI disruption risk to FIDO2 or e-signature workflows, or whether competitor activity in AI-native authentication is accelerating.
red_team.critique: OneSpan's regulatory moat (PSD2/eIDAS) is real, but the claim of 'structural insulation' goes unvalidated. If OSPN's filings flag AI authentication as an emerging competitive threat, or if competitors like Okta or Auth0 have begun integrating AI-native features into their authentication offerings, the -2.83% five-day move may reflect legitimate competitive pressure rather than sympathy fade. The flag provides no evidence from OSPN's recent earnings or competitive positioning to support the 'insulation' thesis.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity — more compute = smaller geometries = more inspection passes per wafer. A Gemini-on-Android release has no mechanical link to wafer fab inspection demand. The sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base.
entries.what_kills: Memory market downturn affecting Onto's specialty-semi exposure; delay or unfavorable terms on the Rigaku transaction; broader semi-cap capex air pocket.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger; threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. Process-control metrology for advanced semiconductor nodes is actually benefited by AI-chip manufacturing complexity. Move -5.9%, five-day +15.37% (already recovered sharply, light volume 0.75x). Part of separate semi-cap trigger (not the 7-flag Google-Gemini consumer-OS basket)—this is Android-17-as-driver-of-semi-demand, only 2 flags (ONTO, AEIS) on this specific thesis. Basket rule does NOT apply (only 2 flags). Should be exploratory on its merits: threat='minimal', confidence=4, clean filings story (AI as capex tailwind).

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day change of +15.37% directly contradicts the claim that the drop is a 'clean overshoot'—the stock has already recovered sharply, eliminating the alpha from a sympathy-fade entry at this price.
red_team.critique: The thesis that process-control metrology benefits from AI-chip complexity is sound, but the timing critique is fatal: ONTO is up 15.37% over five days, meaning most or all of the sympathy-fade overshoot has already been recovered. Entering an 'exploratory' position after a 15% five-day recovery on light volume (0.75x) is poor entry discipline—the easy money has been made. The flag's own 'setup' narrative ('clean overshoot') is negated by the positive five-day performance, suggesting the panic was both shallow and brief.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: AEIS's own 10-K identifies AI infrastructure as a material growth driver. Power conversion for fabs and AI data centers is structurally long the buildout that Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude inference all require. A consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery demand. The sympathy drop on weak volume with a still-positive five-day looks like a clean overshoot.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction; loss of design-win share to a competitor; Data Center Computing margins continuing to compress as that segment grows.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger (semi-capex thesis); threat_assessment='none', confidence=4, panic_calibration='unjustified'. 10-K explicitly states AI infrastructure is a revenue tailwind and states accelerated capacity investments. Move -5.45%, five-day +12.45% (already recovered, weak volume 0.67x). Only 2 flags on this semi-capex-positive thesis (ONTO, AEIS) → basket rule does NOT apply. AEIS has threat='none' (stronger than ONTO's 'minimal') and explicit 10-K evidence of AI as growth driver. Could justify conviction tier, but position has already recovered 12%+ and thesis is better served as exploratory test position to capture any further washout. Size at exploratory (6%).

red_team.weakest_link: Like ONTO, AEIS has already recovered 12.45% over five days, eliminating the alpha from a sympathy-fade entry; the flag explicitly acknowledges this recovery but then still recommends an exploratory entry, which is inconsistent with best-entry discipline.
red_team.critique: AEIS's threat='none' and explicit 10-K evidence that AI infrastructure is a growth driver are correct and strong. However, the five-day recovery of +12.45% on weak volume (0.67x) means the sympathy-fade opportunity has already been largely captured by early buyers. The flag's own statement—'position has already recovered 12%+ and thesis is better served as exploratory test position to capture any further washout'—is wishful thinking; there is no evidence of further washout, and entry here is chasing the rebound rather than buying the panic. At down only -5.45% on the day with a 12% five-day recovery, AEIS is no longer an 'overdone' opportunity.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to a consumer Android+Gemini release. Their AI inference offerings (GPU droplets, GenAI platform) actually align them with the trend. The mechanical link from 'Google ships Gemini in Android' to 'DigitalOcean's SMB cloud subscriptions decline' is nonexistent.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler price-cuts on small instances making DOCN's pricing edge less meaningful; AI/ML revenue traction failing to materialize in next quarter; SMB churn acceleration.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger; threat_assessment='minimal', confidence=3 (at threshold), panic_calibration='unjustified'. SMB cloud orthogonal to consumer Android release; DOCN's own filings list AI/ML as a growth opportunity, not a threat. Move -3.92%, five-day +3.48% (light volume 0.52x, not extended). Confidence at minimum threshold, but threat and calibration are clean. Part of separate basket from 7-flag Google-Gemini consumer-app basket (this is infrastructure/platform, not consumer-facing). Only 1 flag on this specific narrative → basket rule does not apply. Exploratory tier justified on merits.

red_team.weakest_link: Confidence in the decision is set at the minimum threshold (3), and the flag does not verify whether DOCN's recent 10-K or 10-Q actually lists AI/ML as a growth opportunity or whether those revenue streams are material enough to offset any SMB cloud pressure.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on the claim that 'DOCN's own filings list AI/ML as a growth opportunity,' but this is stated without citation or verification. If AI/ML is a marginal revenue line item (<5% of total revenue), then the upside thesis is overstated. Additionally, the flag describes this as a 'separate basket' with only 1 flag, suggesting low conviction across the broader thesis. The 5-day move of +3.48% (already positive) on minimal volume (0.52x) suggests the market views this as immaterial; entering an exploratory position in DOCN after it has already recovered 3.48% in five days is poor timing relative to the sympathy-fade thesis.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
entries.thesis: An Anthropic regulatory ban is the opposite of a sympathy-fade catalyst for nCino — if anything, it signals AI deployment friction that protects incumbent regulated-workflow vendors. nCino's filings show genuine AI/ML competitive pressure, but a US government ban on a specific lab's models doesn't accelerate that pressure; it slows it. Multi-year subscription revenue recognition also means today's news cannot meaningfully change the next several quarters of revenue.
entries.what_kills: A major bank publicly switches from nCino to an LLM-based internal system, or a competitor announces a credible AI-native loan origination platform with regulatory blessing. The filings explicitly warn this is the long-term risk.
entries.reasoning: Anthropic-ban trigger; confidence=3 (at min threshold), threat_assessment='indirect' (above 'minimal'). Filings show genuine AI/ML competitive exposure, and Anthropic-ban arguably helps nCino by slowing competitive AI deployment on regulated bank workflows. Thesis is less clean than pure 'sympathy fade'—bank risk factor is real, not noise. Part of 3-flag Anthropic-ban basket → would be exploratory, but confidence exactly at threshold and threat indirect. WATCH pending clearer filings evidence or volume follow-through.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A consumer Gemini speaker has no overlap with payroll tax compliance, but PAYC's filings do reveal a real second-order AI risk (employee headcount compression at customers). Move is partial — five-day decline already extended. The Google Home trigger specifically is not the right driver; this is broader AI-on-HR-software anxiety.
entries.what_kills: Material guide-down on per-employee pricing or client headcount; a major HR-AI agent launch from Microsoft/Google specifically targeting SMB payroll; further breakdown of the already-extended 5-day chart.
entries.reasoning: Google-Gemini trigger; confidence=3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Filings explicitly flag real AI/ML risk: employee-headcount displacement reducing per-employee revenue. This is not pure sympathy fade—there is genuine second-order exposure. Move -4.19%, five-day already -9.51% (extended). Basket rule applies (7 Gemini flags), but confidence at floor and threat/panic are 'partial'/'indirect', making this less clean than other basket entries. WATCH pending further evidence of employment-level compression or HR-AI competitive launch.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Same logic as PAYC — Google Home doesn't file 941s or handle state UI registrations. However the move is on top of an already-extended 5-day decline, suggesting the broader HR/payroll AI fear is in motion. Sympathy fade is mis-targeted to this specific Gemini trigger.
entries.what_kills: An AI-native payroll competitor gaining material SMB traction; client headcount compression; further leg down on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Google-Gemini trigger; confidence=3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Similar to PAYC—filings warn of AI-driven HR-software disruption risk, but Google Home release is not the direct driver. Move -3.64%, five-day -9.97% (extended). Part of 7-flag basket; confidence at threshold and threat indirect → WATCH rather than exploratory BUY.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: The Google Home speaker trigger is unrelated to language learning, but DUOL has real (though manageable) exposure to consumer AI products. The dopamine-loop/streak moat is the defense, and DUOL is monetizing AI via Duolingo Max. Move on light volume looks more like consumer-AI basket fade than fundamental threat.
entries.what_kills: A flagship consumer AI tutor product (ChatGPT Tutor, Gemini Tutor) gaining material DAU traction; declining DAUs or streak metrics; mobile platform fee or policy changes from Apple/Google.
entries.reasoning: Google-Gemini / Android 17 trigger; confidence=3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Filings explicitly flag GenAI as competitive threat to language-learning business, though DUOL has launched Duolingo Max to monetize the trend. Move -3.65%, five-day +1.21% (not extended, light volume). This is real (not misread) competitive pressure, not pure sympathy fade. Part of 7-flag basket; confidence at floor and threat real → WATCH.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer OS release adding Gemini features to Android phones has zero mechanical connection to photomask demand. If anything, more AI compute = more advanced-node chip designs = more photomask demand. The selloff is pure thematic semi-sympathy tape with the sign flipped — Photronics is a beneficiary of AI capex, not a victim of a consumer mobile feature release.
entries.what_kills: An actual cyclical downturn in semi capex unrelated to today's trigger; loss of a top-two customer; further Section 232 / China export restrictions that materially reduce PLAB's addressable Asia revenue (82% of total).
entries.reasoning: Already held in portfolio (8 days, +15.09%, open Screen 1 position). Duplicate flag on same name does not trigger new BUY decision. Re-flag is confirming thesis but position is live and recovering; no new action warranted.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: HCAT has plenty of real issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, end-market funding cuts) but a consumer Android Gemini release is not one of them. HIPAA, BAA requirements, and EHR integration depth prevent general-purpose AI agents from touching provider data. The drop is sympathy-fade on top of an already-pressured name. Confidence intentionally limited because HCAT has genuine, name-specific issues that make this less clean.
entries.what_kills: Further Medicaid funding cuts pressuring health-system IT spend; client losses to Epic/Oracle accelerating; Ignite migration causing more revenue churn than guided.
entries.reasoning: Google-Gemini trigger; confidence=2 (below min_buy_confidence=3). HCAT has genuine, name-specific operational issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, Medicaid funding risk) that make sympathy-fade thesis less clean. Confidence floor not met.
